scope: Publication claim governance mapped to canonical validation artifacts

claims.claim: Validation stack is reproducible with explicit claim tiers and provenance boundaries.
claims.required_wording: Use wording equivalent to reproducible computational validation with explicit claim-level governance.
claims.forbidden_wording: Do not imply full experimental reproducibility across external labs without independent reruns.

claims.claim: Task1 1Mb setup confirms strong Pearson agreement (>0.5) with Hi-C.
claims.required_wording: State that Pearson > 0.5 hypothesis is rejected in current setup; keep benchmark interpretation exploratory.
claims.forbidden_wording: Any statement that Pearson target was achieved in Task1.

claims.claim: Task1 provides limited structural signal via LSSIM under current setup.
claims.required_wording: Use 'supported with limits' and mention setup constraints.
claims.forbidden_wording: Do not claim publication-grade benchmark superiority.

claims.claim: Loop That Stayed is proven as a pathogenic class.
claims.required_wording: State UNVERIFIED for pathogenic class status; frame as unresolved hypothesis.
claims.forbidden_wording: Any definitive mechanistic or clinical proof statement.

claims.claim: Task2 supports model-discordance observation between structural and sequence predictors.
claims.required_wording: Keep statement at observational model-discordance level.
claims.forbidden_wording: Do not escalate to causal disease mechanism or clinical utility.

claims.claim: Task3 weak-CTCF no-effect finding is supported in-model.
claims.required_wording: Include explicit 'SUPPORTED_IN_MODEL' and external validation disclaimer.
claims.forbidden_wording: Do not present as externally validated biology.
Any causal or clinical impact claim from Task4 alone.

claims.claim: Task5 enables clinical reclassification of VUS.
claims.required_wording: State clinical reclassification as UNVERIFIED / NO_GO under current criteria.
claims.forbidden_wording: Any direct clinical deployment claim based on Task5 metrics.

claims.claim: Task5 provides reproducible computational stratification for hypothesis generation.
